steps.title: Find the independent clause
steps.content: The sentence has a complete independent clause: Exemplars of Stigler's law abound. What follows is a list of examples from multiple fields.

steps.title: Place the colon correctly
steps.content:
A colon after a complete independent clause introduces what follows.

All list items must come after the colon. Choice C places the colon immediately after abound, keeping the entire list together.

Choice B includes in mathematics before the colon, which breaks the list structure and creates a semantic mismatch (claiming exemplars abound in one field, then listing examples from all fields).

Choices A and D use incorrect punctuation. That matches choice C — C is correct.
